behaviors.description: Storing a PASS review summary is blocked when any stored dimension finding for the same round has a FAIL verdict.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Integration (summary-write path must read stored findings for the same round). PASS2-AUDIT: Unit rejected because failure is at module handoff (summary persistence + findings repository query), not one pure function. System rejected by behavior text "stored ... for the same round" (no required real OS/network/subprocess). Agentic/Workflow rejected because no model-output dependence or multi-agent sequence is described. FINAL: Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Wire summary service + findings repository (test DB). Seed round R with at least one FAIL dimension finding, then attempt to store PASS summary for round R. Assert write is rejected and no PASS state is persisted.

behaviors.description: Stop-gate verdict computation reads per-dimension stored findings directly and does not rely only on hand-written summary text.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Integration (gate module + storage module interaction). PASS2-AUDIT: Unit rejected because this behavior is specifically about data source selection across modules (stored findings vs summary text). System rejected: no real HTTP/subprocess/OS primitive required by behavior text. Agentic/Workflow rejected: verdict correctness here comes from deterministic stored verdicts, not model judgment. FINAL: Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Use real gate computation path with repository adapters against local test DB. Store contradictory data (summary says PASS, stored findings include FAIL). Run stop-gate computation and assert FAIL/block result is derived from stored findings, not summary text.

behaviors.description: Manual summary text overrides require an explicit override flag and produce an auditable warning record.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Integration (override validation + audit record writer). PASS2-AUDIT: Unit rejected because behavior requires side effect in audit subsystem, not only flag parsing. System rejected by behavior text: "auditable warning record" can be validated in local DB/log sink without real external infra. Agentic/Workflow rejected: no model-dependent quality check. FINAL: Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Exercise summary submission API/service in two cases: (1) override text without override flag -> reject; (2) with override flag -> accept and persist warning/audit record containing actor, round, reason, timestamp. Assert both policy and audit side effect.

behaviors.description: After Phase 4 fixes, the gate requires a review round identifier produced after the latest fix commit before PASS can be finalized.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Integration (gate logic must compare fix-commit metadata with review-round metadata). PASS2-AUDIT: Unit rejected because this is cross-module state handoff (commit tracker + review rounds + gate). System considered then rejected: behavior text requires ordering relation ("produced after the latest fix commit"), not real git subprocess semantics; in-process metadata fixtures can reproduce failure. Agentic/Workflow rejected: dependence is on timestamps/IDs, not model output content. FINAL: Integration.
behaviors.test_description: With test DB, create commit C2 as latest fix commit and two review rounds R1(before C2) and R2(after C2). Verify PASS finalization is blocked with only R1 and allowed only when R2 identifier is provided/linked.

behaviors.description: In the live review loop, PASS cannot finalize unless a fresh review-agent round runs on the updated diff.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Agentic (behavior explicitly requires a real review-agent round in the live loop). PASS2-AUDIT: Integration considered via stubbed round records, but rejected because behavior text says "live review loop" and "review-agent round runs on the updated diff"; a stub can hide failures where real agent execution/update-diff coupling is broken. Workflow considered, then rejected: this behavior requires one fresh agentic round gate, not multiple sequential model decisions. FINAL: Agentic.
behaviors.test_description: Run the live loop against a real review-agent call on updated diff D2. Attempt PASS finalization after only pre-fix round on D1 -> blocked. Trigger actual new review-agent round on D2 -> PASS eligibility can proceed (subject to findings). Assert gate binds to fresh real round execution metadata.

behaviors.description: If review rounds are exhausted with unresolved MUST-FIX findings in stored results, the outcome is escalation rather than PASS.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Integration (round counter + findings store + decision policy). PASS2-AUDIT: Unit rejected because exhaustion state and unresolved findings are combined across modules/state. System rejected: no required real OS/network/subprocess in behavior text. Agentic/Workflow rejected because condition is computed from stored MUST-FIX verdicts; no dependence on fresh model output. FINAL: Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Seed N rounds exhausted and at least one unresolved MUST-FIX in stored results. Execute decision engine and assert outcome is escalation status, PASS is blocked, and escalation reason references unresolved MUST-FIX.

behaviors.description: In a live multi-round review workflow, exhausted rounds with unresolved MUST-FIX findings still block PASS and emit escalation.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Workflow (explicitly "live multi-round review workflow" with repeated review-agent rounds + terminal gate decision). PASS2-AUDIT: Agentic considered, but rejected because behavior requires sequence semantics across multiple rounds/exhaustion, not a single model call. Integration rejected by behavior text "live multi-round ... workflow"; stubbed state can miss cross-round handoff bugs. FINAL: Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end live loop across multiple real review-agent rounds until configured max rounds. Keep at least one MUST-FIX unresolved in stored findings through final round. Assert PASS remains blocked and escalation event/record is emitted at exhaustion boundary.
